findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 48. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: A decorative colored horizontal bar/divider appears in the HTML between the outline section and the History/Background section that does not appear in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 (HTML page showing outline and History/Background) has no colored bar between sections, but the HTML shows a gradient green-blue-dark bar between the outline and Section I.

findings.description: Fabricated email address in the digital signature block. The HTML contains a mailto hyperlink 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov' but the PDF shows the email as 'elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov' split across lines. While the intent may be correct, the HTML constructs a clickable mailto link that is not present in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML: elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov. PDF shows: 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov, c=US' as part of a digital signature stamp image/text.

findings.description: Fabricated hyperlinks in table of contents. The HTML adds internal anchor links (#history-background, #march-16-2016-public-meeting-and-oral-comments-summary, etc.) to the table of contents items. The PDF has no such hyperlinks; these are plain text items in a numbered list.
findings.evidence: HTML: History/Background, etc. PDF: plain text outline 'I. History/Background' with no links.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements and the document title 'MEMORANDUM' is used as h1 while the actual section headings use h2. This is acceptable structurally, but the PDF page header text 'Meeting Officer's Report / Coal Ash Impoundment Classification(s) / Roxboro Steam Electric Plant, Person County' which appears on pages 2-5 is not represented as a document title. The choice of 'MEMORANDUM' as h1 is reasonable but worth noting.
findings.evidence: HTML uses h1 for 'MEMORANDUM' and h2 for section headings I-IV. PDF has 'MEMORANDUM' as a heading on page 1 and running headers on subsequent pages.

findings.description: The HTML contains two nested <main> elements and duplicates the full header/letterhead information. The page 1 content appears to be rendered twice - once in the initial structure and again within the nested main. The memorandum text, To/From/Subject fields, introductory paragraphs, and table of contents all appear in both the header area and the nested main element.
findings.evidence:
The HTML has
containing the full memo details (To, From, Subject, intro paragraph, TOC), followed by a second
that repeats the same content starting with

MEMORANDUM

and all the same fields.

findings.description: An unexplained embedded image tag appears between the table of contents and Section I, which seems to be an artifact (possibly a page break or decorative element from the PDF conversion).
findings.evidence: HTML: appears between the TOC and Section I with empty alt text.
